ratings.comment: Excellent notes, very helpful. Some accent.
Excellent notes, very accessible and helping professor - always ready and eager to help

He's hard to understand and goes through lecture slides really fast.  I found it best to look at slides before class, take notes, and then take more in class.  Exam is difficult, but if you go to his review session and study A LOT, you can get an A
Excellent teacher; knows his subject. Only problem is accent (sometimes).
Lectures are straight out of the book, he reads off the slides, no point in going, just study the slides and memorize them to get an A

He was not a very good professor at all. Read directly from the slides and was a very boring lecturer overall. You would ask him questions in the review and instead of answering them he'd just say "Oh, you don't need to know that."

Aight, here's the deal... this guy teaches very poorly. going to lecture is worthless because they're posted on the website, and he doesn't say much more than what's written on the slides. his exam is very easy! you get to write on a note card, and his exam comes directly from the lecutures. take the class, don't go, and ace it.
Powerpoints are identical to book material.  Makes the subject dull, and is hard to understand because of accent.  Tests are very hard, even if you study.

His exam was extremely specific and his review session was a joke.  His lectures were simply him reading from powerpoint and he was very difficult to understand when he didn't read from them.  One must rely heavily on the book and know most of the material on his slides.
